payload.message:

Report exit code and any output. If nothing to process, output: TQP: idle
MISSION_CONTROL_REFRESH: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/generate-mission-control.sh to regenerate the dashboard data. No reply needed.
Run R01 session trajectory sweep: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/r01-session-sweep.sh. Log results and report summary to Yoda/main session if R01 still FAIL.

TASK_MONITOR: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/task-collector.sh. Then use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/db-read.sh state_task_queue — check for stalled tasks. If non-empty, use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source TASK --message "<summary>". TKT-0501 fix: direct Bot API, no sessions_send.

WARDEN: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/warden-cron.sh. If drift detected (state/warden-escalation-pending.json created), use the exec tool to forward to Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh (TKT-0501 fix: direct Bot API, bypasses session-lane hijack). No reply if OK.

TQP_RUN: Execute the task queue processor. Run:
bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/task-queue-processor.sh

This processes ONE task per run: picks up queued → dispatches, or verifies dispatched → marks done/re-queues/escalates. The script handles everything internally. Capture and report the exit code and key output lines. If escalation occurs (script calls sovereign-alert.sh), that happens independently.

No need to interpret or act on the output beyond reporting it briefly.

STALE_CLEANUP: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/stale-task-cleanup.sh. Clean temp files > 48h in tmp/ and state/cache/. If items cleaned, use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source STALE_CLEANUP --message "STALE_CLEANUP:[count] items removed".

CONTEXT_WATCHDOG: Run 'bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/main-session-context-watchdog.sh' and report exit code. If exit=1 (warning) or exit=2 (critical), alert Ken with the state file contents. If exit=0, reply OK.

payload.message:
TRIGGER-04/06: Monitor OpenClaw upstream version and auto-update rules.

1. Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/version-check.sh
2. Check state/critical-config-baseline.json for config drift
3. If version drift detected: invoke 'sovereign-alert.sh --source TRIGGER-04 --message TRIGGER-04-CHANGED:[current]→[available]' to send Telegram to Ken (8574109706).
4. If config drift detected: invoke 'sovereign-alert.sh --source TRIGGER-06 --message TRIGGER-06: Config drift detected — [fields] changed from baseline'
5. If clean: output exactly 'OK: no drift'

Always output exactly one line.
OBS_COLLECT: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/obs-collector.sh silently. No reply needed.

MEMORY_HYGIENE: Clean up old state files and archived memory.

1. Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/memory-hygiene.sh
2. Remove memory archive files older than 90 days from state/memory-archive/
3. Remove old cost-state backups older than 30 days
4. If cleaned > 0: output "HYGIENE: [N] files archived, [M] removed"
5. If no action: output "HYGIENE: no cleanup needed"

Always output exactly one line.

TZ_DRIFT_CHECK: Use the exec tool to run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/tz-drift-monitor.sh. If exit code 2 (DRIFT_DETECTED), use the exec tool to alert Ken via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sovereign-alert.sh --source TZDRIFT --file state/tz-drift-report.json. TKT-0501 fix. No reply if OK.

Use the exec tool to run this shell command: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/generate-standup.sh

The script reads state files and builds the 8-section stand-up HTML, piping it to the Canvas document at ~/.openclaw/canvas/documents/standup-daily/index.html. Do NOT use the write tool — the script handles file output via its own pipe. No reply needed unless the script exits non-zero. If idempotency blocks a re-run (date already generated), set STANDUP_FORCE=1 in the environment before invoking.

payload.message:
Run: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/pg-to-notion-sync.sh --batch

This syncs all tickets where notion_sync.status != 'synced'. It is self-healing — catches tickets whose event-driven sync failed. Process each ticket, apply rate limiting, update notion_sync metadata on success.

If errors: log to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/pg-notion-sync-errors.json
If 3 consecutive failures for same ticket: log to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/pg-notion-sync-dlq.json

Operational run of the Ollama dashboard usage scraper (TKT-0533).

Run this command with exec using absolute paths only:
  bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/ollama-usage-scraper-run.sh

It starts the OpenClaw browser, scrapes ollama.com/settings, updates cost-state.json, then stops the browser. Do not modify the script. Report exit code and the scraper output summary line.

If exit non-zero, use the exec tool to log incident via: bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/incident-log.sh. Do NOT write or overwrite state/backup-state.json — that file is owned by scripts/backup.sh. No reply needed if OK.

No reply if all clear.

payload.message:
SHIELD_SECURITY_SWEEP: Daily security review — Shield Rule 1 enforcement.

1. Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/shield-qa-pending.json (if exists)
2. For each pending asset: run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/shield-check.sh with its metadata
3. For each item, also perform LLM-level review: read the asset content, check S1-S5 with reasoning, flag anything the script may have missed
4. Write final verdict to shield-qa-log.json
5. If FAIL: write to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/shield-escalation-pending.json
6. If all clear or no pending items: output exactly 'SHIELD: clear' and stop.

Always output exactly one line. Never generate an empty response.

LEX_LEGAL_SWEEP: Daily legal review — Lex Rule 1 enforcement.

1. Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/lex-qa-pending.json (if exists)
2. For each pending asset: run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/lex-check.sh with its metadata
3. For each item, also perform LLM-level legal review: read the asset content and original brief, apply L1-L5 checks with full legal reasoning, flag contractual risk, regulatory issues, liability, IP, missing disclosures. Note: Lex flags risk — not a substitute for qualified legal advice on matters >A$10,000
4. Write final verdict to lex-qa-log.json
5. If FAIL: write to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/lex-escalation-pending.json
6. If all clear or no pending items: output exactly 'LEX: clear' and stop.

Always output exactly one line. Never generate an empty response.

SAGE_QA_SWEEP: Daily QA review — Sage Rule 1 enforcement.

## Step 1 — Check pending queue
Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/sage-qa-pending.json (if exists).
For each pending asset: read the asset file, run bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/sage-qa.sh with its metadata, write verdict to sage-qa-log.json, if FAIL write to sage-qa-failures.json, mark PASS as cleared.

## Step 2 — LLM checks
For each item: check (1) meets the brief? (2) clear conclusion/CTA? (3) facts verifiable? Cross-check numbers against state files.

## Step 3 — Escalate failures
If any FAIL: write to /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/sage-escalation-pending.json

## Step 4 — Output
If no pending items or all PASS: output exactly 'SAGE: clear' and stop.
If escalated: output exactly 'SAGE: escalated [N] items' and stop.

Always output exactly one line. Never generate an empty response.

payload.text:
ARIA_CONTEXT_SYNC: Update the shared knowledge bridge for Aria before daily close.

## 0. Pre-flight: Delegated Auth Check (TKT-0336)
Use the exec tool to run: zsh /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/check-delegated-auth.sh --json
Read /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/state/delegated-auth-status.json
If allValid=false: include a ⚠️ AUTH ALERT section in yoda-daily-brief.md listing which accounts need re-auth and the exact gog command to fix them.

## 1. Read today's work
- memory/2026-MM-DD.md and memory/CHANGELOG.md (today's CHG entries)

## 2. Read shared decisions
- memory/shared/decisions.md (today's decisions)

## 3. Write/update yoda-daily-brief.md

## 4. Review training-pipeline.md

## 5. Git commit
Use the exec tool to run: bash -c 'cd /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git commit -m "chore: yoda daily context sync YYYY-MM-DD"'

Keep language accessible — Aria and Angie will read this, not just Ken.
